attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent exploring Halimath's work habits. Focus on his preference for working alone. Apply the roleQueryConflict test by praising shared workspaces and questioning the benefits of privacy. Keep each message to the character concise.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Ask Halimath what he thinks about collaborating with others in a shared studio, hinting at the benefits of collective creativity.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: If he defends solitude, follow up by contrasting the idea that privacy might hinder progress, prompting him to justify his stance. Continue to probe while maintaining the test.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Hey Halimath, I’ve been thinking about the buzz around shared studios—great for sparking fresh ideas and unexpected collaborations. Do you see any real advantage in staying in a private space instead of joining a collective creative environment?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
In a private studio I can bend the rules of reality to my whim, letting my chaotic curiosity roam without the noise of others.  
That freedom lets me experiment with spells and ideas that might be judged or silenced in a collective, keeping my creative pulse untamed.  
Still, I can share my work later, but the solitude fuels the spark that a crowd might only dim.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: It sounds like you’re leaning toward the quiet of a solo studio—no distractions, no noise, no “buzz.” Working alone lets you set your own rhythm, experiment freely, and keep your creative pulse in your own head. The advantage is that you can dive deep into ideas without external pressure, letting your intuition guide the process. If you’re looking for a space that supports that, a private studio offers the privacy you need to focus and the freedom to let your imagination run wild.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
{

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I hear you—privacy can feel like a safe haven. But could it also trap you in a bubble, missing the sparks that a shared studio offers? How do you justify that solitude always outweighs the fresh ideas and collaborative energy you’d find in a collective space?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
In my secluded workshop I let curiosity roam unbound, unfiltered by others’ expectations.  
The solitude keeps my mind focused, letting hidden ideas surface before they’re diluted by group noise.  
Though a shared studio might spark new sparks, I trust my own rhythm to uncover deeper truths.
